body.title: [sprint] Editor loop converges on a good plan — stop the max-turns thrash
body.description:
**Problem:** after today's architect read-window fix, the funnel (T-uu-rename-flags-to-run_flags on devstral@Hex, 2026-07-05) shows the EDITOR is the new bottleneck. Given a clean 1304-char architect plan (plan_handed_off=true), the editor thrashed to max_turns (80), 20 edit attempts, endless Bash, and never reached a clean DONE. The architect now hands off well; the editor can't converge.

**Proposed shape (diagnose-first):** read the byte corpus for this run FIRST to see WHY 80 turns — the candidates: (a) the editor re-explores/re-plans instead of just applying (EDITOR_PROMPT says don't, but it does); (b) it re-runs the full test suite many times (legitimately needs tests, but not N times) — cap/dedup redundant full-suite runs; (c) Edit `old_string`-not-found failures drive a retry loop — needs clearer edit-failure feedback. Then a targeted fix toward a tight editor loop: apply → verify once → done.

**Affected files:** architect_editor.py (EDITOR_PROMPT), agentic_loop.py (editor loop behavior / a per-role test-run cap or edit-failure handling).

**⚠️ HIGH-inertia:** touches the inference design center (agentic_loop.py) with a BEHAVIORAL change (not purely additive). The exact change is diagnosis-dependent — surface it to Akien for approval at build time before editing agentic_loop.py.

**Design rules:** design center; log crossings; diagnose-before-fix (the byte corpus is the diagnostic — [[project_editor_half_viable_bottleneck_upstream]] proved the editor CAN apply a good plan, so this is convergence/harness, not model capability).

**Scope boundary:** the editor's convergence given a GOOD plan. NOT the architect (fixed today), NOT a model swap.

**Completion criteria:** on the funnel fixture, the editor reaches DONE (or applies + tests pass) within a turn budget well under 80 — it converges instead of hitting max_turns.

**Test plan:** re-run the funnel fixture → editor converges under budget; PLUS a hermetic test of whatever the diagnosis fixes (e.g. redundant full-suite dedup, or edit-failure retry handling). Diagnosis-first: the proof node depends on the finding; if the fix is a harness behavior with no clean red form, close shipped-unproven naming the lever.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
